reference: My dad has a prostate-specific antigen value of fourteen point nine eight. He has been advised to take Microdox LBX lactic bacillus. After a month, he is to take the prostate-specific antigen test again. If the prostate-specific antigen value is high, then he is advised to take a biopsy. He had a prior prostate surgery. Can I know whether his prostate-specific antigen value is high because of prior surgery? His prostate is a little bulged. Please reply.
text: If the software detects a bug, it will notify you.
